notes: Runs flawlessly. Since the native Linux version has some limitations (3rd party middleware used for cloth simulation is not available under Linux, hence e.g. no capes are shown on the characters), this is one of the cases where the Windows version run using Proton feels more complete and immersive than the native Linux one.
title: Pillars of Eternity

notes: The native linux version would install but not run at all. It would just start and close instantly with no errors or explanations as to why. There are many complaints online about it. When forced to install through proton it runs perfectly though. It synced up to my windows saved games as well with no issues.
title: Pillars of Eternity

notes: Game works native and on Proton out of the box. I installed manually D9VK and it worked as well fine with this DX9 to Vulkan translation, except for text rendering.
title: Pillars of Eternity

notes: Game works native on Linux. On Proton it works out of the box. I installed manually D9VK and it worked as well fine with this DX9 to Vulkan translation, only some minor shadow glitches.
title: Pillars of Eternity
